questions.question: Why use a declarative YAML constitution rather than ad-hoc code checks?
options: Because YAML is faster to parse than Python
Because rules in YAML are reviewable by non-engineers, lived in version control, and replaceable without a code change
Because the standard library prefers YAML

questions.question: What fields must every rule have at minimum?
How does the engine compose predicates?
options: Only via a single contains_regex per rule
Via all_of, any_of, and not_ which recursively wrap atomic predicates

questions.question: What is the role of applies_when?
options: It gates the must predicate so rules that do not apply are recorded as not_applicable rather than violation

questions.question: What does the Fixer do?
options: It applies the per-rule declarative fix (append, prepend, replace_regex) to produce a revised text
It computes severity from the fix
It calls a remote model to repair the text

questions.question: Why does the demo run the engine a second time after the fixer?
options: To verify the revised text satisfies the rules and to surface any rule the fixer cannot repair locally
Because the first pass is only a dry run
